tasks.title: Add planRestricted column to accommodations and owner_promotions (DB)
tasks.description: Realign D-3: new boolean column `plan_restricted` (default false, NOT NULL) on BOTH `accommodations` and `owner_promotions` tables in packages/db schema. Do NOT reuse ownerSuspended (pause-flow collision). Generate versioned migration via `pnpm db:generate` (drift guard requires the committed migration file). Promotions need their own marker because a lifecycle flip would lose the 'restricted-by-plan' info needed for selective restore. Tests: model-level defaults; migration applies cleanly on the worktree test DB.

tasks.title: Add planRestricted to accommodation + promotion Zod schemas and types
tasks.description: @repo/schemas: add `planRestricted: z.boolean().default(false)` to the accommodation and owner-promotion entity schemas (find via entity-name.schema.ts convention). Regenerate/extend inferred types. Lesson SPEC-145/149: schema package export changes can break 'already green' consumers — note for the sweep. Tests: schema parse defaults; round-trip with the new field.

tasks.title: Add archivedGallery to media Zod schema
tasks.description: Realign D-2: add optional `archivedGallery` (same item shape as `gallery`) to the accommodation media JSONB Zod schema in @repo/schemas. NO DB migration (JSONB). Public/API read paths only consume `gallery` so zero read changes — VERIFY that claim with a grep for media.gallery consumers and note any that iterate all keys. Tests: parse with/without archivedGallery; existing media fixtures unaffected.

tasks.title: Exclude planRestricted accommodations from public read paths
tasks.description: Visibility predicate becomes published && !ownerSuspended && !planRestricted. Audit every public accommodation query (list/detail/search/destination pages) — find where ownerSuspended is filtered today and mirror. Also exclude planRestricted promotions from public/active reads AND from the MAX_ACTIVE_PROMOTIONS count in apps/api/src/middlewares/limit-enforcement.ts (restricted ≠ active). Tests: public list excludes restricted; detail of restricted returns 404/not-visible consistent with ownerSuspended behavior; limit counter ignores restricted promotions.

tasks.title: Red-first unit tests for computeDowngradeExcess
tasks.description: Write the failing test suite for the excess-diff helper BEFORE implementing (spec §3 dimensions): accommodations over MAX_ACCOMMODATIONS, active promotions over MAX_ACTIVE_PROMOTIONS, photos per accommodation over MAX_PHOTOS_PER_ACCOMMODATION, rich-description and video grandfather detection. Cases: under-cap (empty diff), exactly-at-cap, over-cap with default keep selection (most-recently-updated first), unlimited target (no limit key). Test file colocated with subscription-downgrade.service tests.

tasks.title: Implement computeDowngradeExcess (read-only diff helper)
tasks.description: In apps/api/src/services/subscription-downgrade.service.ts (or sibling module if >500 lines): RO-RO helper computing usage-vs-target excess for all §3 dimensions + the default keep selection (most-recently-updated; most-viewed as tiebreaker if view data is cheap to reach). Read-only — NO mutations. Returns a structured preview object (this shape IS the SPEC-203 preview contract — export its Zod schema from @repo/schemas). Make T-005 tests green.

tasks.title: Accommodation restrict/restore primitives
tasks.description: Service primitives: restrictAccommodations({ ids }) sets planRestricted=true; restoreAccommodations({ ids }) unsets. Through @repo/db models (BaseModel), no raw SQL. Both return affected ids. INV-5: no other field touched, nothing deleted. Unit tests: set/unset round-trip, empty ids no-op, ownerSuspended untouched.

tasks.title: Promotion restrict/restore primitives
tasks.description: restrictPromotions({ ids }) sets planRestricted=true (lifecycle stays untouched — the flag excludes them from active counts/reads per T-004); restorePromotions({ ids }) unsets. No batch-deactivate exists today (realign finding 6) — build on OwnerPromotionService/model. Unit tests: round-trip, lifecycle untouched, restricted excluded from active count.

tasks.title: Photo archive/restore primitive (JSONB gallery move)
tasks.description: archiveAccommodationPhotos({ accommodationId, keepIds }) moves non-kept gallery items from media.gallery to media.archivedGallery; restoreAccommodationPhotos moves back (respecting target cap on restore). featuredImage counts toward the cap but is always kept by default. INV-5: items moved, never dropped — assert total count invariant in tests. Unit tests: move/restore round-trip preserves every item byte-for-byte, keepIds honored, cap edge cases.

tasks.title: Add targeted batch revalidation helper
tasks.description: Realign B-2: packages/service-core/src/revalidation/revalidation.service.ts has scheduleRevalidation (per-entity, debounced) and revalidateByEntityType (ALL of a type — too broad). Add scheduleRevalidationBatch({ events }) for a targeted id list (loop with debounce reuse is fine; keep fire-and-forget semantics). Unit tests: N events scheduled, debounce key per entity preserved.

tasks.title: Shared restriction service applyDowngradeRestrictions (red-first)
tasks.description: Realign D-1: THE shared service both the cron and the admin hook call. applyDowngradeRestrictions({ customerId, targetPlanId, selections? }): recompute excess FRESH (T-006), merge host selections (keepIds) with defaults, call primitives (T-007/008/009), schedule batch revalidation (T-010). IDEMPOTENT: second run with same state is a no-op (already-restricted items don't double-count). Red-first unit tests: full flow per dimension, selections honored, idempotency, under-cap no-op, partial failure surfaces an error (no silent swallow — SPEC-149 lesson).

tasks.title: Restore-on-upgrade service + wiring into upgrade paths
tasks.description: applyUpgradeRestorations({ customerId, newPlanId }): restore plan-restricted resources up to the new caps (most-recently-restricted first if still capped; photos restored within cap). Wire into the paid-upgrade success path (initiatePaidPlanUpgrade post-success in the upgrade service/route) and leave a callable for the admin hook (T-014). Unit tests: full restore when new plan uncapped, partial restore at cap, no-op when nothing restricted.

tasks.title: Wire restriction into apply-scheduled-plan-changes cron
tasks.description: apps/api/src/cron/jobs/apply-scheduled-plan-changes.ts: after changePlan succeeds for a DOWNGRADE (step between plan apply and cache clear), call applyDowngradeRestrictions with persisted selections (T-015). Respect the SPEC-194 pre-stamp idempotency (restriction failure must NOT roll back the applied plan change — instead mark the run result.success=false so SPEC-149's cron soft-failure Sentry capture fires; document the recovery path). Unit tests on the cron with restriction mock: called for downgrades only, failure → soft-fail not throw, selections passed through.

tasks.title: Wire restriction + restoration into admin change-plan hook
tasks.description: Realign D-1: apps/api/src/routes/billing/admin/qzpay-admin-hooks.ts onAfterSubscriptionChangePlan (~line 349) — admin plan changes are IMMEDIATE. Compare old vs new plan: downgrade → applyDowngradeRestrictions synchronously (no selections — default keep policy); upgrade → applyUpgradeRestorations. Keep existing audit-log + clearEntitlementCache untouched. Unit tests: both directions, same-plan no-op, restriction error does not break the admin response (log + Sentry, hook contract preserved — check how other hooks handle errors).

tasks.title: keepIds selection field: schema + persistence with scheduled change
tasks.description: @repo/schemas: extend the plan-change downgrade request schema with optional keepSelections ({ accommodationIds?, promotionIds?, photoKeepMap? } — align shape with the T-006 preview contract). Persist with the QZPayScheduledPlanChange record (check what metadata/JSON field the qzpay scheduled-change row supports; if none, a sidecar table/column is needed — prefer the simplest carrier that survives until apply). Unit tests: schema validation, persistence round-trip, replacedPriorSchedule overwrites selections.

tasks.title: Request-time restriction preview in plan-change downgrade response
tasks.description: apps/api/src/routes/billing/plan-change.ts downgrade branch (lines ~348-405): include the computeDowngradeExcess preview in the scheduled response + accept/persist keepSelections (T-015). ⚠️ SPEC-149 catch lives in the OUTERMOST catch (~415-431): new code goes inside the inner try; new error types must map BEFORE isBillingProviderError or they 500. Unit tests: preview shape for over-cap host, empty preview under cap, selections persisted, REGRESSION: provider-error mapping (429→503 etc.) still works on this route (re-run plan-change-provider-error.test.ts).

tasks.title: Wire downgrade notifications (warning at schedule, confirmation at apply)
tasks.description: Realign B-1: types + templates EXIST in packages/notifications but are NEVER sent. Wire: (1) PLAN_DOWNGRADE_LIMIT_WARNING at schedule time when the preview shows excess (include what-will-be-restricted summary + deep link placeholder for SPEC-203); (2) PLAN_CHANGE_CONFIRMATION at apply time from the cron (template props: recipientName, oldPlanName, newPlanName, renewalDate). Unit tests with notification mocks: sent exactly once per event, NOT sent under cap, send failure is logged + soft, never blocks the flow.

tasks.title: Fix pause/resume revalidation gap (spec §6)
tasks.description: Realign C confirmed: apps/api/src/services/subscription-pause.service.ts setOwnerServiceSuspension flips accommodations.ownerSuspended directly with NO scheduleRevalidation — stale Cloudflare ISR pages keep showing paused listings. Call scheduleRevalidationBatch (T-010) with the affected accommodation events on suspend AND resume. Regression tests: revalidation scheduled with the right ids both directions; DB write behavior unchanged.

tasks.title: e2e: downgrade restriction happy path
tasks.description: New e2e in apps/api/test/e2e/flows/billing/ (closes the T-145-11 gap — no placeholder exists): over-cap host (3 accommodations/cap 1, active promos over cap, photos over cap) schedules downgrade → response carries preview → cron applies → accommodations planRestricted + promos restricted + photos archived → public list/detail exclude restricted → entitlement cache cleared. Use billing-factories + mp-stub harness (SPEC-143). Follow the plan-downgrade-cron.test.ts patterns.

tasks.title: e2e: keepIds honored + restore-on-upgrade + idempotent re-apply
tasks.description: Same e2e harness: (1) host schedules downgrade WITH keepSelections → cron applies → exactly the selected items stay active; (2) re-upgrade → restricted resources restored (planRestricted unset, photos back in gallery); (3) cron re-run of an applied change does NOT double-restrict (idempotency with the SPEC-194 pre-stamp).

tasks.title: INV-5 pinning + non-downgrade regression tests
tasks.description: Pinning suite (SPEC-149 style): (1) INV-5 — restrict+restore round-trip leaves every resource byte-identical (no deletes anywhere in the restriction path: assert row counts + media item counts); (2) upgrades and same-plan changes NEVER invoke restriction; (3) pause/resume does NOT touch planRestricted (D-3 separation pin). These protect the policy decisions against future refactors.

tasks.title: Verification sweep (typecheck, biome, touched + consumer tests, e2e)
tasks.description: Re-run EVERYTHING touched plus consumers of changed @repo/schemas exports (lesson SPEC-145/149: 'already green' files break when schemas gain exports). Per-package typecheck (api, schemas, db, service-core), biome check on the diff, every touched unit test file sequentially, the 3 billing e2e suites (only pre-existing failures allowed: monthly :506/:771 webhook activation, annual public:read). Rebuild stale dists before believing typecheck errors (lesson SPEC-149 post-merge).

tasks.title: Adversarial fresh-context review + apply confirmed fixes
tasks.description: Fresh-context review (opus) of the full diff. Hunt list: INV-5 violations (anything destructive), restriction/restore asymmetries (restore misses a dimension restrict covers), idempotency holes (double-restrict on retry), selection-vs-default merge bugs, visibility predicate gaps (a public path missing the planRestricted filter = revenue leak persists), revalidation misses, notification PII, SPEC-149 catch collision in plan-change.ts. Apply confirmed fixes via fix agent; re-run affected tests.

tasks.title: Register staging smoke entry #6 (SPEC-193 batch)
tasks.description: Billing CORE change (downgrade/plan-change flow + cron) → MUST register deferred smoke sections in .qtm/specs/SPEC-193-billing-go-live-readiness-master/docs/pending-staging-smoke.md (entry #6, with PR number): scheduled downgrade with over-cap host on staging MP sandbox → preview in response → cron applies → restriction visible (public page excluded post-revalidation) → re-upgrade restores → notifications received. Follow entries #1-5 format.

tasks.title: PR to staging + CI + merge + closeout
tasks.description: Resync with origin/staging first (keep-both on .qtm index conflicts; rebuild dists post-merge before trusting typecheck). PR to staging referencing the smoke entry. CI Pass SUCCESS gate (gh pr view N --json statusCheckRollup; push via /usr/bin/git push + verify rev-parse local==remote — rtk no-ops silently). Merge --merge. Closeout: spec.md + specs/index.json + tasks/index.json completed+archived in the SAME change (index sync rules), engram state save, ask owner OK for worktree cleanup.

tasks.title: Fix getByDestination visibility gap (ownerSuspended + planRestricted)
tasks.description: Absorbed pre-existing bug (owner approved 2026-06-06, found in T-004 audit): accommodation getByDestination uses raw findAll WITHOUT the ownerSuspended filter — suspended (and now plan-restricted) listings leak through destination pages. Fix: apply the same public visibility predicate (ownerSuspended=false AND planRestricted=false, with the same isOwnScope/VIP exemptions as search) to getByDestination in accommodation.service/model. Regression test reproducing the leak FIRST (bug-fix rule), then fix. Re-run the T-004 test files.
